entities.name: RHIZOCTONIA BATATICOLA
entities.description: Sclerotial stage of Macrophomina phaseolina, a pathogen causing charcoal rot disease in groundnut

entities.name: MACROPHOMINA PHASEOLINA
entities.description: A fungal pathogen that causes charcoal rot disease in groundnut

entities.name: CHARCOAL ROT DISEASE
entities.description: A disease of groundnut caused by the pathogen Rhizoctonia bataticola

entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: An agricultural crop affected by charcoal rot disease and used in seed storage studies

entities.name: WARM AND WET CONDITIONS
entities.description: Environmental field conditions that facilitate the entry of the pathogen into groundnut seeds

entities.name: MEDIUM TERM STORAGE
entities.description: A storage condition for groundnut germplasm, typically at 4°C

entities.name: 20°C
entities.description: A storage temperature used to study the survival of Rhizoctonia bataticola in groundnut seeds

entities.name: 4°C
entities.description: A storage temperature used to study the survival of Rhizoctonia bataticola in groundnut seeds

entities.name: -18°C
entities.description: A storage temperature used to study the survival of Rhizoctonia bataticola in groundnut seeds, recommended for long term storage

entities.name: SEED GERMINATION
entities.description: The ability of groundnut seeds to sprout, which is reduced by the presence of Rhizoctonia bataticola

entities.name: PATHOGEN INFECTION
entities.description: The incidence and presence of Rhizoctonia bataticola within groundnut seeds

entities.name: AGAR PLATE METHOD
entities.description: A laboratory method used for the detection of the pathogen Rhizoctonia bataticola

entities.name: PAPER TOWEL METHOD
entities.description: A laboratory method used to determine the germination rate of groundnut seeds

entities.name: SURVIVAL
entities.description: The ability of the pathogen Rhizoctonia bataticola to persist in groundnut seeds over time

entities.name: LONGEVITY
entities.description: The duration for which the pathogen Rhizoctonia bataticola remains viable in groundnut seeds
